revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)]][[ja:甲塚古墳 (いわき市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)]][[ja:甲塚古墳 (いわき市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)]][[ja:甲塚古墳 (いわき市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwaki)]][[ja:甲塚古墳 (いわき市)]]
{{Short description|Burial site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | native_name =甲塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Kabutoduka Kohun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|37.053194|N|140.941917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Iwaki|qid=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = 37 meters | circumference = | height = | built = 5th to 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period|Kofun]] | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Kabutozuka Kofun '''|甲塚古墳|Kabutozuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in what is now the city of {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|qid=Q274384|lt=Iwaki|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1923.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/190192|title=甲塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The ''kofun'' is located on flat ground near the mouth of Natsui River, surrounded by rice pa11ies. The area has a dense concentration of ruins from the [[Kofun period]] through the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, and the Kabutoyama Kofun is the only survivor of a cluster of tumuli which were destroyed by local farmers over the years to increase cultivatable land. During the construction of the nearby {{ill|Japan National Route 6|en|Japan National Route 6|qid=Q1038714|lt=Japan National Route 6|1=Japan National Route 6}} Bypass, the remains of nine tumuli, along with a large number of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, wooden markers and ritual objects, and other artifacts have been found.
The Kabutozuka Kofun is a well-preserved large {{nihongo|{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=''empun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style megalithic tomb with a diameter of 37 meters and a height of 8.2 meters, taking its name from its resemblance to a ''{{ill|Kabuto|en|Kabuto|qid=Q1720878|lt=Kabuto|1=Kabuto}}'' helmet. The tomb has not been excavated, so its internal structure and age is not known, but it is estimated to date from the late 6th to late 7th centuries by the artifacts which we found in the vicinity. This area was near the center of ancient Iwaki county and near the Iwaki county administrative center, so it can be postulated that it was a tomb of the ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of ancient {{ill|Iwaki Province (718)|en|Iwaki Province (718)|qid=Q1192809|lt=Iwaki Province|1=Iwaki Province (718)}}.
It is located approximately 40 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kusano Station (Fukushima)|en|Kusano Station (Fukushima)|qid=Q2496615|lt=Kusano Station|1=Kusano Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|qid=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|qid=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
